raw_output_payload.reasoning: Need find files under /testbed containing FooBar in path. Then copy to current dir preserving attributes: cp -p? but need preserve ownership requires sudo? use cp -a? but only files. Use find /testbed -type f -path '*FooBar*' -exec cp -p {} . \; . That preserves mode, ownership? -p preserves mode, ownership, timestamps. But need preserve ownership requires root. Use cp -a? but -a includes directories. We'll use cp -p. So command: find /testbed -type f -path '*FooBar*' -exec cp -p {} . \;

So command: find /testbed -type f -path '*FooBar*' -exec cp -p {} . \;
